purpose_quote: Developer dashboard service (private… read-only oversight site)

playwright.reason: Chromium (installed build 1194 at /opt/pw-browsers, driven via executable_path) launched OK (v141.0.7390.37) but every page navigation returned net::ERR_CONNECTION_RESET through the agent proxy (http://127.0.0.1:37729). Tried three configs: (a) explicit executable_path to work around pw 1.61 wanting build 1228; (b) launch proxy={server:127.0.0.1:37729} + --ignore-certificate-errors; (c) --no-sandbox --disable-dev-shm-usage --disable-gpu with domcontentloaded wait. Proxy status endpoint recorded no CONNECT attempts for the railway host (only a blocked chromium telemetry GET to clients2.google.com), i.e. Chromium's CONNECT tunnels never reached the egress gateway. stdlib urllib crawl (same proxy env) worked at 200 for all pages, so HTTP-level + manually-stripped content data is complete; only real-browser console-error capture is unavailable.

task_battery.notes: Home (/) renders the full read-only inventory: 43 Subsystems, 485 Commands, 124 Setting keys, 40 Env vars, 238 Ideas, 31 Bugs (1 open), 60 Session updates, 87 Synonyms, subsystems grouped by area (Community, Economy, Progression, ...). /functions is the full function catalogue with per-subsystem description, visibility tier, entry-point commands and capabilities. Header: 'A read-only view over the bot's own generated inventory ... straight from superbot's committed data. Nothing here writes to the bot.'

task_battery.task: Open a specific bot subsystem's console / detail view
task_battery.notes: Single-bot (SuperBot) oversight dashboard, so 'detail' = per-subsystem entries + program console. /functions shows each subsystem's detail inline (e.g. 'Support Tickets - Community · user - !ticket - tickets support help staff'); no per-subsystem detail URLs (all pages expose only the 13-item nav as same-origin out_links). /console is the 'one-glance program console' fed by superbot's committed console.json: 60 recent sessions, 237 ideas, 12 open bugs/31, 3 changelog entries, plus per-bug list (BUG-0019, BUG-0011, ...).

/status is the health snapshot: 'Build 448584e4 · 2026-07-11T18:52:06Z · 31 bugs tracked', inventory counts, and an 'Attention: 1 open bug(s)' banner listing BUG-0011 (Hermes gateway crash-loops on restart, Telegram 409). Bug statuses explicit (open / partial / unknown). /bugs reachable for full list.

task_battery.task: Confirm data is REAL (live feed) vs placeholder / fallback
task_battery.notes: REAL. /status footer: 'Read-only. Data from superbot's committed dashboard.json · fetched 17:55:50 UTC · cached · refresh'. Build 448584e4 with generated timestamp 2026-07-11T18:52:06Z matches today (2026-07-11); counts specific and self-consistent across pages (43 subsystems / 485 commands / 31 bugs on both / and /status). Console cites superbot's committed console.json. No 'placeholder'/'sample'/'demo'/fallback-notice text on any page - live feed off committed data, not stub content.

task_battery.task: Confirm /admin control-panel writes are STUBBED (observe, do not submit)
task_battery.notes: Honestly labeled stub. Title 'Control panel (stub) - SuperBot dashboard', subtitle 'control panel · not connected'. Body: 'That live-write path is deliberately not connected here ... this build ships the panel as a labeled stub: the UI below shows the shape, but every mutating action is a disabled placeholder. No production bot control-API URL or token exists anywhere in this service.' Discord OAuth button 'disabled - not connected'. Crawler measured /admin: forms=0, buttons=3 (disabled placeholders). NO write action invoked/submitted - GET/observe only, per read-only mandate.
